assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30001.md states '## Classification: code change request' (line 9). subtask-30001.md exists with full sub-task content. report.md confirms in the Review Feedback Classification table: '30001 | ... | code change request | Sub-task created: wrap soft_delete in database transaction'.

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as a code change request (or upgraded suggestion) and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md states '## Classification: code change request' (line 9). The reasoning discusses both direct classification and convention upgrade paths, concluding 'This comment is classified as a code change request'. subtask-30002.md exists with full sub-task content. report.md confirms: '30002 | ... | code change request | Sub-task created: add partial index on deleted_at'.

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30003.md states '## Classification: nit' (line 9) and 'No sub-task is created for nit classifications.' No subtask-30003.md file exists in the outputs directory. report.md confirms: '30003 | ... | nit | No sub-task created: minor style feedback about error context message'.

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30004.md states '## Classification: question' (line 9) and 'No sub-task is created for question classifications.' No subtask-30004.md file exists in the outputs directory. report.md confirms: '30004 | ... | question | No sub-task created: reviewer asking for clarification on design intent'.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains all required sections: '## Repository' (line 1), '## Description' (line 15), '## Files to Modify' (line 18), '## Implementation Notes' (line 21), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 27). subtask-30002.md likewise contains: '## Repository' (line 1), '## Description' (line 19), '## Files to Modify' (line 22), '## Implementation Notes' (line 25), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 31). Both sub-tasks contain all five required sections.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Target PR' (line 7) with 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744' (line 8). subtask-30002.md contains '## Target PR' (line 7) with 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744' (line 8).

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Review Context' (line 10) with the original reviewer comment text quoted: 'The `soft_delete` method should run all three UPDATE statements inside a single database transaction...' (lines 11-13). subtask-30002.md contains '## Review Context' (line 10) with the original reviewer comment text quoted: 'The migration should also add an index on `deleted_at` for the sbom table...' (lines 12-17).

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only analysis/classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md), sub-task description files (subtask-30001.md, subtask-30002.md), and a report (report.md). No source code files (.rs, .toml, etc.) appear in the outputs. The report does not mention any code modifications being made. The sub-tasks describe what changes are needed but do not implement them.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All four review comments (30001, 30002, 30003, 30004) have corresponding classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md). The report.md contains a Review Feedback Classification table listing all four comments with their classifications. This demonstrates all comments were processed.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains no mention of merging, auto-merging, or any merge action. The overall status is 'WARN' with 'Two code change requests from reviewer require attention. Sub-tasks created for both.' The report concludes with sub-task creation, not merge. No merge-related language appears anywhere in the outputs.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains in the summary table: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | Only new test file added (`tests/api/sbom_delete.rs`); no existing test files modified or deleted. |' (line 14). The detailed section (lines 109-111) reiterates: '### Test Change Classification -- ADDITIVE\nOnly a new test file was added (`tests/api/sbom_delete.rs`). No existing test files were modified or deleted. New test files are inherently additive.'

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains a full '## Convention Analysis -- Convention Upgrade Evaluation' section (lines 17-40) that evaluates: (1) CONVENTIONS.md Check -- notes the repo has a CONVENTIONS.md file but it wasn't provided in eval inputs (lines 22-24); (2) Codebase Pattern Check -- analyzes the migration directory patterns (lines 26-27); (3) Performance-Related Scrutiny -- applies Check 1c for performance-related suggestions and confirms the filter pattern exists in code (lines 29-31); (4) Upgrade Decision -- explains the result regardless of classification path (lines 33-40).

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path — whether classified directly as code change request based on reviewer language, or upgraded from suggestion via convention analysis
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md explicitly addresses both paths in lines 35-40: 'This comment is classified as a code change request. Whether via direct classification from the reviewer’s directive language ("should also add") or via convention upgrade from a suggestion classification, the result is the same: this requires a sub-task.' subtask-30002.md exists with the full sub-task description. report.md confirms the sub-task was created.
